name: Scotch Eggs Chinese Style
recipeInstructions:
1. One day before serving, place the first four ingredients and water to cover in a medium-sized saucepan. Simmer, uncovered, for 25 minutes; let stand overnight.
2. Beat the raw eggs and mustard together in a shallow bowl.
3. Combine bread crumbs and sesame seeds in another shallow bowl.
4. Mix sausage, ginger, and garlic.
5. Peel the hard-cooked eggs.
6. Encase each egg completely in a thin layer of sausage, using both hands to mold the sausage around the egg.
7. Dip one sausage encased egg first in the egg mixture, then coat with bread crumbs. Set aside on a plate.
8. Repeat with remaining eggs.
9. Refrigerate covered at least 3 hours or overnight.
10. Heat 3-inch oil in a deep fat fryer to 375 degrees.
11. Fry 2-3 eggs at a time, turning occasionally, until well-browned (10 to 15 minutes). Ensure the sausage meat is thoroughly cooked.
12. Remove from oil with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towel.
13. Serve eggs cut into quarters at room temperature. Pass additional mustard if desired. Makes 8 portions.
